context.description: Philosophical prose on the limits of self-knowledge, ego, and mutual misunderstanding. It argues that people often project their own assumptions outward and need a shared measure to judge what each side can or cannot understand.
normalization_notes: Continuous prose page from a German book on consciousness and self-reflection.
Title inferred from source file name: Bewusstseinsreisen.

rows.paragraph_text: People easily deceive, exaggerate, and separate themselves from themselves, mistaking their own ideas for reality.
rows.text_content: People easily deceive, exaggerate, and separate themselves from themselves, mistaking their own ideas for reality.

rows.paragraph_text: Without a shared standard, two people cannot know whether the other is speaking nonsense or whether they are simply hearing nonsense because they do not understand.
rows.text_content: Without a shared standard, two people cannot know whether the other is speaking nonsense or whether they are simply hearing nonsense because they do not understand.

rows.paragraph_text: The text uses IQ comparisons to show how knowledge gaps can be obvious to one person and deeply offensive or invisible to another.
rows.text_content: The text uses IQ comparisons to show how knowledge gaps can be obvious to one person and deeply offensive or invisible to another.

content.free_text: book_page nonfiction_philosophy philosophy_of_consciousness Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen, page 95, in German. Philosophical reflection on consciousness, ego, self-deception, and the difficulty of mutual understanding without a shared standard.
